# UC-21.4.12 · Traffic Management System Sensor Availability

## Description

Roadside sensor availability impacts traffic management system accuracy. Monitoring sensor uptime enables proactive maintenance and reliable traffic data.

## Value

Service and risk leaders use signals from Traffic Management System Sensor Availability to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.

## Implementation

Monitor sensor heartbeats. Tune stale threshold per sensor class. Alert maintenance when availability drops below target. **Domain context:** ATMS data feeds ITS and traveler information systems—stale speed/occupancy sensors degrade routing algorithms and public-facing travel times. **Splunk:** `stats avg(online)` across all rows per sensor assumes one row per poll—if multiple rows per sensor per bucket, use `latest(online)` or `timechart` instead.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: ATMS field device gateway via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=logistics` `sourcetype="tms:sensor"` fields `sensor_id`, `last_reading_epoch`, `status`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Monitor sensor heartbeats. Tune stale threshold per sensor class. Alert maintenance when availability drops below target. **Domain context:** ATMS data feeds ITS and traveler information systems—stale speed/occupancy sensors degrade routing algorithms and public-facing travel times. **Splunk:** `stats avg(online)` across all rows per sensor assumes one row per poll—if multiple rows per sensor per bucket, use `latest(online)` or `timechart` instead.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=logistics sourcetype="tms:sensor"
| eval age_sec=now()-last_reading_epoch
| eval online=if(status="active" AND age_sec < 300, 1, 0)
| stats avg(online) as avail_frac by sensor_id
| eval avail_pct=round(avail_frac*100,2)
| where avail_pct < 95
| table sensor_id, avail_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Traffic Management System Sensor Availability** — Roadside sensor availability impacts traffic management system accuracy. Monitoring sensor uptime enables proactive maintenance and reliable traffic data.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=logistics` `sourcetype="tms:sensor"` fields `sensor_id`, `last_reading_epoch`, `status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): ATMS field device gateway via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: logistics; **sourcetype**: tms:sensor. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=logistics, sourcetype="tms:sensor". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **online** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sensor_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **avail_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where avail_pct < 95` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Traffic Management System Sensor Availability**): table sensor_id, avail_pct


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the fleet telematics or TMS portal and, for warehouse or port flows, the WMS or terminal operations screen for the same vehicle, container, or order window. Align GPS time zones and odometer units with the source system.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Map (sensor status), Table (offline sensors), Time chart (online %).

## SPL

```spl
index=logistics sourcetype="tms:sensor"
| eval age_sec=now()-last_reading_epoch
| eval online=if(status="active" AND age_sec < 300, 1, 0)
| stats avg(online) as avail_frac by sensor_id
| eval avail_pct=round(avail_frac*100,2)
| where avail_pct < 95
| table sensor_id, avail_pct
```

## Visualization

Map (sensor status), Table (offline sensors), Time chart (online %).
